nodes.label: Tandem
nodes.rationale: Multi-account task delegation for Things 3: tag a todo with a family member's name and it appears natively in their Things Inbox. Fills a gap Cultured Code leaves open (no public API, no multi-user sync).

nodes.label: Hub (stateful coordinator)
nodes.rationale: The only stateful component: id-mapping ledger (SQLite WAL), tenants/members/devices with capability tiers, durable delivery queue with leases, hashed device-token auth. Never touches Things. Stdlib-only Python.

nodes.label: Spoke (per-member agent)
nodes.rationale: Thin, near-stateless agent per member Mac: reads Things via a read-only snapshot mirror, pushes outbound-tagged todos, applies inbound deliveries via the sanctioned things:///json URL scheme, echoes terminal states. Runs on Apple's stock /usr/bin/python3.

nodes.label: No-loss invariant
nodes.rationale: Sender's copy is never modified until the hub durably committed the transfer; deliveries persist until acked; leases expire and re-queue. The tag itself is the retry queue when the hub is down.

nodes.label: No-dup invariant
nodes.rationale: Idempotent push (UNIQUE(tenant, from, src_uuid, rev)), idempotent queueing (UNIQUE(transfer, kind, to_member)), and a spoke-side intent journal that re-correlates instead of re-firing after a crash.

nodes.label: Completion round-trip invariant
nodes.rationale: Both sides of every open transfer are watched; terminal state is set once at the hub (completed beats canceled); the echo is just another idempotent delivery.

nodes.label: Sanctioned Things URL scheme
nodes.rationale: Writes go through Cultured Code's own documented URL scheme; Tandem never touches Things Cloud and never asks for a password — the safety argument against credential-grabbing third-party tools.

nodes.label: Hub-and-spoke architecture
nodes.rationale: P2P was rejected because an id-mapping ledger needs one durable home. Hub is relocatable by construction: stdlib HTTP + SQLite in a configurable state dir, zero host-specific paths.

nodes.label: In-process write gateway (deployment amendment)
nodes.rationale: Amended 2026-07-11: for a member whose infrastructure already runs an always-on Things write gateway, the hub runs the spoke core in-process (Syncthing-synced mirror for reads, op queue for writes). Only members without a gateway get a real LaunchAgent spoke. Same SpokeCore, different backends — sync semantics cannot diverge because the code is shared.

nodes.label: Delegation sync flow
nodes.rationale: Six steps: trigger tag → push transfer (idempotent) → recipient leases, journals intent, applies create, correlates, acks with dst_uuid → sender retags + completes own copy (D2) → recipient completes whenever → echo delivery as race-safety net.

nodes.label: D2: delegating IS the action
nodes.rationale: Amended 2026-07-11: the sender's copy is marked completed the moment delivery is confirmed, not when the recipient eventually finishes. Removes sender revocation entirely (no window), and the completion echo becomes a race-safety net that always lands on completed, never canceled — no downgrade path.

nodes.label: Exact-match trigger tags
nodes.rationale: Trigger tags match exact configured titles (case-insensitive), never substrings — a pre-existing decorative tag containing a member's name must not trigger delegation.

nodes.label: Spoke tick loop
nodes.rationale: Serialized tick on a configurable interval (5s as deployed): refresh mirror → outbound push → inbound apply one-at-a-time → observe terminals → retag+complete sender copies. Things itself is the outbound queue; the hub is the inbound queue — no spoke-side outbound queue.

nodes.label: Crash-safety intent journal
nodes.rationale: A tiny local SQLite journal records create-apply intent before firing the write; on restart the spoke re-runs correlation and re-acks instead of re-firing. Residual double-failure window degrades to one visible duplicate, never silent loss.

nodes.label: Ledger schema
nodes.rationale: tenants / members (capability tiers) / devices (sha256 token hashes) / transfers (src_uuid↔dst_uuid, rev seam, terminal, payload envelope) / deliveries (queued→leased→done, lease expiry) / events (append-only audit). Idempotency anchors: UNIQUE(tenant_id, from_member, src_uuid, rev) and UNIQUE(transfer_id, kind, to_member).

nodes.label: Envelope fidelity contract
nodes.rationale: Schema-versioned tandem.todo/1. Title and notes byte-identical — no sync markers ever injected (identity lives in the ledger's uuid mapping). Control tags never cross the wire; payload tags dropped in v1; recipient gets exactly one pre-created provenance tag from-<sender-handle>.

nodes.label: Device-token auth and Principal
nodes.rationale: Bearer device-token resolves to Principal(tenant_id, member_id, device_id, caps); the sync core never sees tokens. Hub stores only hashes; tenant isolation is structural — every table roots at tenant_id and no API accepts a tenant parameter from a client.

nodes.label: Long-poll push transport
nodes.rationale: Only inbound delivery is a genuine server→client push problem, so long-poll (wait≤30s) expresses it completely. WebSocket/SSE rejected: they add framing + proxy-idle-timeout keepalive for zero extra capability, and long-poll survives Cloudflare Tunnel/Tailscale untouched. Measured push-commit→return ~2ms.

nodes.label: Delivery condition variable
nodes.rationale: Ledger holds a threading.Condition with its own lock and a monotonic generation counter; every path committing a leasable delivery signals AFTER the transaction commits (signal-after-commit is load-bearing — waking before commit reintroduces a latency floor). One primitive serves both the HTTP long-poll handler and the in-process gateway worker.

nodes.label: Tick phase decoupling (tick_local / tick_inbound)
nodes.rationale: A held inbound long-poll would starve outbound/observe in one serial tick, so SpokeCore.tick() splits into tick_local() and tick_inbound(). LAN spokes run one loop; the gateway runs two loops with SpokeState made thread-safe by a private RLock.

nodes.label: Git-repo-as-transport (rejected)
nodes.rationale: The predecessor system's merge/reset/push-retry machinery was a third of the code and its likeliest failure surface; the HTTP hub + transactional ledger dissolves it. Also rejected: sync markers in notes, full bidirectional merge (delegation = single writer dissolves conflicts), direct SQLite writes, auto-created Sync Inbox project.

nodes.label: tandem.todo/1 wire envelope
nodes.rationale: JSON: title (required), notes (byte-identical ≤10000), checklist, when (yyyy-mm-dd|someday|null), deadline, context_url (reserved seam, null in v1). No tags in the payload — provenance tag is computed by the hub, not carried.

nodes.label: Transfer state machine
nodes.rationale: created → applied (create acked with dst_uuid) → terminal ∈ {completed, canceled} → resolved (echo acked). Terminal is set-once with one sanctioned upgrade: canceled → completed (work done wins), only while the cancel echo hasn't been delivered.

nodes.label: Delivery state machine
nodes.rationale: queued → leased (GET /v1/deliveries) → done (ack); nack or lease expiry re-queues. Ordering guards: a terminal delivery is never handed over before the transfer has a dst_uuid; a terminal echo replaces a still-queued create when the sender revoked first.

nodes.label: POST /v1/transfers
nodes.rationale: Requires can_send. 201 with the transfer record, or 200 with deduped:true on replay — idempotent on (from_member, src_uuid, rev).

nodes.label: GET /v1/deliveries (lease + long-poll)
nodes.rationale: Requires can_receive. Leases up to N deliveries, long-polls up to 30s, lease TTL 300s with expiry re-queue. Ack (create kind requires dst_uuid — closes the uuid mapping) and nack are idempotent.

nodes.label: GET /v1/watch
nodes.rationale: Returns open transfers the caller is party to with role and state; senders use state:applied as the retag (delivery-receipt) signal.

nodes.label: POST /v1/observations
nodes.rationale: Report a terminal state seen on a watched copy (trashed reports as canceled); set-once semantics, always returns the winning terminal.

nodes.label: At-least-once delivery semantics
nodes.rationale: Every retry path is absorbed by an idempotency anchor: transfer replays by natural key, delivery queueing by (transfer, kind, member), applies by the spoke journal, acks by done-state no-ops, observations by set-once terminal. The one residual (journal lost AND ack lost) degrades to a single visible duplicate.

nodes.label: Edit propagation (rev > 1 seam)
nodes.rationale: Sender as sole editor until terminal state; the natural-key uniqueness already anchors it. Ships together with re-delegation of resolved transfers.

nodes.label: Things Cloud protocol spike
nodes.rationale: If the hub spoke Things Cloud directly per account, spokes disappear entirely (no Mac agent, iOS-only members possible). Gated hard: a 2026-07 write-fidelity experiment confirmed core CRUD safe but found one silent-ghost-write defect class; read-only adoption (a change feed replacing spoke polling) is the safe first step.

nodes.label: Per-tenant tag mapping (v2 seam)
nodes.rationale: A per-tenant table translating payload tags between members' vocabularies; v1 drops payload tags because the recipient's tag vocabulary is theirs.

nodes.label: E2E payload encryption (seam)
nodes.rationale: Hub relays ciphertext; the envelope is already an opaque JSON document to the delivery machinery.

nodes.label: TMTask table semantics
nodes.rationale: Verified field semantics: type (0 todo/1 project/2 heading), status (0 open/2 canceled/3 completed), trashed orthogonal to status, start (0 Inbox/1 Anytime-Today/2 Someday), creationDate/userModificationDate are plain Unix epoch seconds (NOT Core Data reference-date offset — a natural trap), rt1_recurrenceRule marks repeating templates.

nodes.label: Packed integer date format
nodes.rationale: startDate/deadline pack Y/M/D into one integer YYYYYYYYYYYMMMMDDDDD0000000; unpacked with the same bitmask expression things.py documents.

nodes.label: Tags must pre-exist for programmatic writes
nodes.rationale: Unknown tags are silently dropped, never created; neither the URL scheme nor the JSON command format can create a tag — only the GUI and AppleScript. This is why deploy scripts pre-create the trigger/delegated/provenance tags.
nodes.norm_label: tags must pre-exist for programmatic writes

nodes.label: things:///json batch write path
nodes.rationale: Batch create/update ops in one open; applies are async fire-and-forget so always verify by reading the DB back (bounded poll). No delete exists in the URL scheme. Repeating todos cannot be completed/updated via it. Things Cloud rate-limits rapid writes (observed 429).

nodes.label: Things self-activation focus steal
nodes.rationale: Things.app can enter a state where it steals macOS focus on any write; open -g does not prevent it. The only working technique is reactive: capture the frontmost app, watch a settle window, knock back observed steals with verify-and-retry.

nodes.label: VACUUM INTO snapshot mirror
nodes.rationale: The Things DB is TCC-protected; spokes read a VACUUM INTO snapshot mirror instead — transactionally consistent, atomically replaced. Direct writes to the DB fork Things Cloud state and are forbidden forever.

nodes.label: graphify knowledge-graph integration
nodes.rationale: Project instruction: answer codebase questions via graphify query/path/explain against graphify-out/graph.json first; run graphify update after code changes.

nodes.label: CI test matrix (ubuntu 3.13 / macos 3.9)
nodes.rationale: Runs python3 -m unittest discover tests on push/PR; macOS pin at Python 3.9 enforces the Apple stock /usr/bin/python3 floor the spoke targets.

nodes.label: Aaron off-LAN spoke setup
nodes.rationale: deploy/jill's pattern adapted for an off-LAN member: hub URL is a public Cloudflare Tunnel hostname instead of a LAN IP, otherwise identical. Unresolved as of 2026-07-11: who actually runs the install on Aaron's Mac (Bradley has no remote access to it).

nodes.label: Cloudflare Tunnel hub access
nodes.rationale: Off-LAN members reach the hub through a Cloudflare Tunnel; long-poll survives the tunnel untouched. Blocked as of 2026-07-11 on a Cloudflare API token permission.

nodes.label: Jill LaunchAgent spoke setup
nodes.rationale: The one real spoke in v1: stock macOS, idempotent install.sh, two LaunchAgents (things-mirror 5s snapshot via zsh+FDA; things-team-spoke tick with ~3s long-poll), tokens deploy-derived from 1Password.

nodes.label: Full Disk Access grant on /bin/zsh
nodes.rationale: The mirror agent's interpreter gets the TCC grant; granting FDA to the stable Apple binary /bin/zsh survives all updates.
